title: Juneteenth
text:
On June 17th, 2021, President Biden signed into law the bill that established as a national public holiday June 19th, Juneteenth National Independence Day. Despite Juneteenth’s storied history, the holiday had largely gone unnoticed by non-Black Americans for long. It was the Black Lives Matter movement that pushed Juneteenth into the national spotlight, following decades of activists’ calls for recognition of the landmark occasion.

In 1863, during the American Civil War between the Union and the Confederacy, President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation, which declared free more than three million slaves living in the Confederate States. More than two years would pass, however, before the news reached African Americans living in Texas. It was not until Union soldiers arrived in Galveston, Texas, on June 19th, 1865 that the town’s residents finally learned that slavery had been abolished. The former slaves immediately began to celebrate with prayer, feasting, song and dance.

The following year, on June 19th, the first official Juneteenth celebrations took place in Texas. They included prayer meetings and the singing of spirituals, and celebrants wore new clothes as a way of representing their newfound freedom.

Since African Americans were prohibited from using public facilities, they often held their annual celebration of freedom at their churches or out in rural areas around rivers and streams, which could provide for additional activities such as fishing, horseback riding and barbecues. There were also visits to the cemeteries to reunite the freed and living with their dead and enslaved ancestors.

Having been commemorated for more than 160 years, Juneteenth has now become a date representing African Americans’ struggle for freedom and equality, as well as an occasion for family and community celebration.

questions.prompt: According to the text, Juneteenth originally celebrated...

options.A: the end of the American Civil War
options.C: the birth of the Black Lives Matter movement
options.D: the emancipation of slaves in Galveston

questions.prompt: According to the text, Juneteenth celebrations were...

options.A: declared a public holiday by President Lincoln

questions.prompt: Non-black Americans have traditionally taken part in Juneteenth events

justification.text: (Despite Juneteenth’s storied history,) the holiday had largely gone unnoticed by non-Black Americans for long.
validTexts: Despite Juneteenth’s storied history, the holiday had largely gone unnoticed by non-Black Americans for long.
the holiday had largely gone unnoticed by non-Black Americans for long.

questions.prompt: The Black Lives Matter movement has played a key role in making Juneteenth a national holiday

justification.text: It was the Black Lives Matter movement that pushed Juneteenth into the national spotlight(, following decades of activists’ calls for recognition of the landmark occasion.)
validTexts: It was the Black Lives Matter movement that pushed Juneteenth into the national spotlight, following decades of activists’ calls for recognition of the landmark occasion.
It was the Black Lives Matter movement that pushed Juneteenth into the national spotlight

questions.prompt: Enslaved people in Galveston were initially reluctant to believe they had been freed

justification.text: The former slaves immediately began to celebrate with prayer, feasting, song and dance.
validTexts: The former slaves immediately began to celebrate with prayer, feasting, song and dance.

questions.prompt: Juneteenth celebration outfits were symbolic of Blacks’ new condition

justification.text: (They included prayer meetings and the singing of spirituals, and) celebrants wore new clothes as a way of representing their newfound freedom.
validTexts: They included prayer meetings and the singing of spirituals, and celebrants wore new clothes as a way of representing their newfound freedom.
celebrants wore new clothes as a way of representing their newfound freedom.

questions.prompt: ONE WORD MEANING: “a place used for a particular activity.”
ONE WORD MEANING: “a social gathering where people come together to enjoy grilled food.”
